topic: An athlete doesn't make the team they expected to join
raw_output:
[story 1]

The crisp autumn air bit at Leo’s exposed skin as he stood by the locker room door, the weight of the rejection settling in his chest like a stone. He’d poured every ounce of his being into those final practices, the grueling early mornings, the sacrifices made for this single opportunity. Now, the official email sat glowing on his phone screen, stark white against the dark background. He reread the line about the roster. It wasn't him. A ripple of heat started in his neck, creeping up to his hairline. He clenched his jaw so tightly his teeth ached. "No," he whispered, the sound barely audible over the distant thud of a basketball. He turned away from the gleaming trophies lining the wall, his gaze snagging on a scuff mark on the polished floor. He took a slow, shaky breath, trying to anchor himself, but his hands were trembling, unable to unclench.

[story 2]

"So, that’s it?" Marcus asked, the words tasting like ash in his mouth. He stared at the coach, his eyes wide and unblinking. He had visualized this moment for months—the handshake, the acceptance speech, the roar of the crowd. Instead, there was just a quiet dismissal. A strange lightness, followed by a heavy, sinking feeling, pooled in his stomach. He started pacing the narrow hallway, his footsteps echoing too loudly in the cavernous space. He stopped abruptly by the window, looking out at the sprawling campus. The sunlight felt too bright, too indifferent. He ran a hand through his already messy hair, a gesture of pure, unmoored confusion. "What did I miss?" he managed, his voice cracking on the last word.

[story 3]

Sarah felt a strange numbness spreading through her limbs. She sat on the edge of the bench, meticulously folding the worn team jacket, though the action felt mechanical, devoid of any real purpose. The news had landed like a physical blow—a sudden, sharp intake of breath that left her lungs feeling tight. She looked down at her hands, noticing the faint tremor that persisted even after she consciously tried to steady them. Her mind cycled through every single reason, every statistic, every perceived flaw, searching desperately for the misplaced variable. When the coach finally walked past, offering a brief, impersonal nod, Sarah didn't move. She just held the folded fabric, her gaze fixed on a distant point, a profound stillness settling over her features as she processed the sudden, unwelcome shift in reality.
